items.title: South Korea and US hold joint exercises as part of annual military drills
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The South Korean and U.S. militaries on Saturday held a joint training exercise at a river in Yeoncheon, north of South Korea's capital, as part of their annual Freedom Shield combined exercise. (AP video shot by Yong-ho Kim)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Police fire tear gas during protest against labor reform in Ecuador
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of workers, students, and teachers protested Friday in Ecuador’s capital Quito after the government extended daily work shift from eight to 10 hours, with a weekly cap of 40 hours. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: AP Exclusive: Turkey FM Hakan Fidan talks diplomatic efforts as war rages
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Turkey’s foreign minister Hakan Fidan said Saturday that there is no serious initiative to resume negotiations between the United States and Iran but that he believes Iran is open to back-channel talks. (AP video by Mehmet Guzel)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: US raises flag at US Embassy in Venezuela for the 1st time since 2019
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The United States flag was raised again at the U.S. Embassy in Caracas on Saturday, seven years after it was lowered when Washington and Caracas cut diplomatic relations in 2019. (AP Video by Juan Arraez).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Trump departs his golf club in West Palm Beach, Florida
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. President Donald Trump departs his golf club in West Palm Beach, as he spends this weekend at Mar-a-Lago. The president was spending Saturday morning at his golf club in Florida, but posted that, “Many Countries, especially those who are affected by Iran’s attempted closure of the Hormuz Strait, will be sending War Ships, in conjunction with the United States of America, to keep the Strait open and safe.”

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Indigenous women witness environmental harm in Ecuador’s Amazon
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Indigenous women from seven Amazon communities visit Ecuador’s northern oil region to raise awareness on the environmental impacts of decades of oil and gas extraction.</article>

items.title: Iran warns UAE about possible attack on US military 'hideouts’
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Iran's joint military command threatened to attack cities in the UAE, home to Dubai and one of the world's busiest airports, saying the U.S. used “ports, docks and hideouts” there to launch strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, without providing evidence. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Indigenous women tour Ecuador oil field to witness environmental harm
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>About 30 Indigenous women from seven Amazon nationalities traveled to Ecuador’s northern oil region to witness the environmental impacts of decades of oil and gas extraction, part of a workshop aimed at preparing communities for possible expansion of drilling. (AP video shot by Cesar Olmos and Steven Grattan).

items.content:

items.title: AP reporters add context to Trump's reasons for attacking Iran
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration has been trying to explain why it struck Iran and killed its supreme leader, and the shifting reasons range from Iran’s nuclear program to its ballistic missiles capabilities.
